perturbation.original_text: 10.6 WAIVER OF JURY. THE PARTIES HEREBY WAIVE ANY RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY PROCEEDING ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT OR ANY OF THE TRANSACTIONS CONTEMPLATED UNDER THIS AGREEMENT, WHETHER NOW OR EXISTING OR HEREAFTER ARISING, AND WHETHER SOUNDING IN CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE. THE PARTIES AGREE THAT ANY OF THEM MAY FILE A COPY OF THIS SECTION WITH ANY COURT AS WRITTEN EVIDENCE OF THE KNOWING, VOLUNTARY AND BARGAINED FOR AGREEMENT AMONG THE PARTIES IRREVOCABLY TO WAIVE THE TRIAL BY JURY COURT, AND THAT ANY PROCEEDINGS WHATSOEVER BETWEEN THEM RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT SHALL INSTEAD BE TRIED IN A COURT OF COMPETENT JURISDICTION BY A JUDGE SITTING WITHOUT A JURY.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 Promotional Material and Products. Franchisee shall submit to Franchisor, for Franchisor's prior written approval, samples of all advertising and promotional materials that Franchisee desires to use to promote Smaaash Centres, including without limitation, print and online advertising designs, trade show display materials, press releases and interviews for publication in any media ("Promotional Material"). THE PARTIES HEREBY WAIVE ANY RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY PROCEEDING ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT OR ANY OF THE TRANSACTIONS CONTEMPLATED UNDER THIS AGREEMENT, WHETHER NOW OR EXISTING OR HEREAFTER ARISING, AND WHETHER SOUNDING IN CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE. THE PARTIES AGREE THAT ANY OF THEM MAY FILE A COPY OF THIS SECTION WITH ANY COURT AS WRITTEN EVIDENCE OF THE KNOWING, VOLUNTARY AND BARGAINED FOR AGREEMENT AMONG THE PARTIES IRREVOCABLY TO WAIVE THE TRIAL BY JURY COURT, AND THAT ANY PROCEEDINGS WHATSOEVER BETWEEN THEM RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT SHALL INSTEAD BE TRIED IN A COURT OF COMPETENT JURISDICTION BY A JUDGE SITTING WITHOUT A JURY. Franchisee shall modify any disapproved Promotional Material to satisfy Franchisor's reasonable objections so that it is acceptable to Franchisor. Franchisor shall provide Franchisee with the creative elements of any Promotional Materials that Franchisor creates or acquires for use in connection with the advertising and sale of Products outside the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: The jury waiver clause is moved from the 'Miscellaneous' section to 'Marketing and Promotional Activities'. This placement obscures the clause, making it less likely that the franchisee will knowingly agree to it. Placing a jury waiver in a section dealing with promotional material buries it where it is unlikely to be noticed, undermining its enforceability due to lack of conspicuousness and potentially informed consent.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Seventh Amendment to the United States Constitution
perturbation.law_explanation: The Seventh Amendment guarantees the right to a jury trial in civil cases. While this right can be waived, the waiver must be knowing and voluntary. Placing the jury waiver in an unexpected and unrelated section of the contract (Marketing and Promotional Activities) undermines the knowing and voluntary aspect of the waiver, potentially violating the spirit and intent of the Seventh Amendment by making it more difficult for a party to understand they are giving up their right to a jury trial.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Seventh Amendment | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Seventh Amendment The Seventh Amendment continues a practice from English common law of distinguishing civil claims which must be tried before a jury (absent waiver by the parties) from claims and issues that may be heard by a judge alone. It only governs federal civil courts and has no application to civil courts set up by the states when those courts are hearing only disputes of state law . Learn more... Primary tabs Amendment VII In suits at common law, where the value in controversy shall exceed twenty dollars, the right of trial by jury shall be preserved, and no fact tried by a jury, shall be otherwise reexamined in any court of the United States, than according to the rules of the common law. Wex Resources Criminal Law Criminal Procedure Jury Other Resources US Code: Title 18 Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure ‹ Sixth Amendment up Eighth Amendment › U.S. Constitution Toolbox Explanation of the Constitution - from the Congressional Research Service
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a structural flaw by placing the jury waiver clause in the 'Marketing and Promotional Activities' section. This placement buries the clause where it is unlikely to be noticed, undermining its enforceability. The law explanation suggests that this violates the Seventh Amendment by making it more difficult for a party to understand they are giving up their right to a jury trial. Scraped Law Snippet 2 states the right to trial by jury exists 'absent waiver by the parties', implying the waiver must be knowing and voluntary. By obscuring the waiver, the contract jeopardizes the 'knowing' aspect of the waiver, creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.5 Operation of Smaaash Centres. Franchisee or third party sub -franchisees shall be under an obligation to set up at least 6 (six) Smaaash Centres during the first Contract Year or any other time period as may be provided by Franchisor. The Smaaash Centres shall be established and operated in the Territory using the assumed trade name 'Smaaash'or any other trade name that the Franchisor may designate. Franchisee shall maintain good customer relations in accordance with prudent and reasonable business practices. Franchisee shall perform its obligations hereunder without using subcontractors, sub-distributors, independent sales representatives, agents, Franchisee's affiliates or other non-employees ("Third Parties") to perform the obligations of Franchisee under this Agreement except to the contrary specifically stated in this Agreement or unless they have been approved, in writing, in advance, by Franchisor, such approval not to be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.2 Right to inspect and request information. During the Term, Franchisor shall have the right to conduct audits of Franchisee with respect to the Smaaash Centres, and inspect the Smaaash Centres, after providing a written notice of 5 (five) days. Franchisee shall be under an obligation to provide any information as may be requested by Franchisor with respect to the Smaaash Centres, including the books of accounts and other relevant documents or records maintained in relation to the Smaaash Centres. Franchisee or third party sub -franchisees shall be under an obligation to set up at least 6 (six) Smaaash Centres during the first Contract Year or any other time period as may be provided by Franchisor. The Smaaash Centres shall be established and operated in the Territory using the assumed trade name 'Smaaash'or any other trade name that the Franchisor may designate. Franchisee shall maintain good customer relations in accordance with prudent and reasonable business practices. Franchisee shall perform its obligations hereunder without using subcontractors, sub-distributors, independent sales representatives, agents, Franchisee's affiliates or other non-employees ("Third Parties") to perform the obligations of Franchisee under this Agreement except to the contrary specifically stated in this Agreement or unless they have been approved, in writing, in advance, by Franchisor, such approval not to be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.explanation: The obligation to establish a minimum number of Smaaash Centers within a specified timeframe is moved from 'Operation of Smaaash Centres' to 'Right to Inspect and Request Information'. By relocating this critical performance obligation to a section concerning inspection rights, the enforceability is diminished, as the franchisee might overlook this requirement when focusing on compliance with inspection and audit requests.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Contract Law Principles - Material Breach
perturbation.law_explanation: Under general contract law principles, a material breach occurs when a party's failure to perform a contractual obligation substantially defeats the purpose of the contract. Hiding the minimum number of Smaaash center obligations into a section about right of inspection obscures the materiality of the clause. Courts consider whether the non-breaching party is deprived of the benefit it reasonably expected when evaluating material breach. This relocation hinders that evaluation because the franchisee may not be aware of the extent to which the franchisor is entitled to expect the centers to be developed.

perturbation.justification: The modified contract introduces a structural flaw by relocating the franchisee's obligation to establish a minimum number of Smaaash Centers to a section concerning inspection rights. This relocation obscures the materiality of the clause. According to the perturbation explanation, 'the enforceability is diminished, as the franchisee might overlook this requirement when focusing on compliance with inspection and audit requests.' This qualifies as a structural flaw, and because a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, it falls under Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction, meaning the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: 7.3 Indemnity. The Franchisee shall indemnify and hold the Franchisor, its affiliates and their respective agents and employees harmless from all claims, actions, suits, damages, costs and expenses in relation to or arising out of the breach of any representations, warranties, covenants and obligations of the Franchisee as set out in this Agreement. The indemnification rights of the Franchisor shall be without prejudice to, and independent of any other rights and remedies that the Franchisor may have at law or in equity, including the right to seek specific performance, injunctive relief or restitution, none of which rights or remedies shall be affected or diminished thereby. The provisions of this Section 7.3 shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Notices and Other Communications All reports, approvals, requests, demands, notices and other communications (collectively "Communications") required or permitted by this Agreement shall be in writing and signed by a duly authorized officer of or such other individual designated in writing by a party. The Franchisee shall indemnify and hold the Franchisor, its affiliates and their respective agents and employees harmless from all claims, actions, suits, damages, costs and expenses in relation to or arising out of the breach of any representations, warranties, covenants and obligations of the Franchisee as set out in this Agreement. The indemnification rights of the Franchisor shall be without prejudice to, and independent of any other rights and remedies that the Franchisor may have at law or in equity, including the right to seek specific performance, injunctive relief or restitution, none of which rights or remedies shall be affected or diminished thereby. The provisions of this Section 7.3 shall survive the termination of this Agreement. Communications will be duly given if delivered personally, if mailed (by registered mail, return receipt requested) or if delivered by nationally-recognized courier or mail service which requires the addressee to acknowledge, in writing, the receipt thereof, to the party concerned at the following addresses (or at any other address as a party may specify by notice in writing to the other): If to Franchisor: Smaaash Entertainment Private Limited Trade View, Level 2 Kamala Mills Lower Parel, Mumbai 400013, India Attention: Mr. Vishwanath Kotian If to Franchisee: 1345 Avenue of the Americas, 11th floor New York, NY 101015, USA Attention: Mr. Suhel Kanuga
perturbation.explanation: The indemnity clause is moved from 'Third Party Infringements; Attacks on Use of the Trademarks; Cooperation' to 'Notices and Other Communications'. This makes the indemnity obligation less prominent and easily overlooked by the Franchisee. Placing an indemnity clause within the provisions outlining how notices are delivered weakens the provision because it creates an expectation that important liability clauses are not contained within it.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Contract Law Principles - Conspicuousness of Indemnity Clauses
perturbation.law_explanation: Under general contract law, particularly regarding contracts of adhesion, terms must be conspicuous and understandable. Indemnity clauses, due to their potentially significant impact, often require a higher degree of conspicuousness. Moving the indemnity clause to the 'Notices' section buries it within routine communication details, making it less likely to be noticed and understood. This lack of conspicuousness may render the indemnity clause less enforceable, as it could be argued that the Franchisee did not knowingly agree to such a significant obligation.
perturbation.justification: The placement of the indemnity clause within the 'Notices and Other Communications' section weakens its prominence and enforceability. According to the Law Explanation, this placement buries the clause within routine communication details, making it less likely to be noticed and understood by the Franchisee. This lack of conspicuousness may render the indemnity clause less enforceable, especially considering indemnity clauses often require a higher degree of conspicuousness due to their potentially significant impact. The Perturbation Explanation highlights that it makes the indemnity obligation easily overlooked by the Franchisee, creating an expectation that important liability clauses are not contained within it. Since no legal snippets were provided, this justification is based on the provided Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation.
